title: The Internet: A Part of Our Lives
passage:
The Internet in Our Daily Lives

The internet is very important today. Many people use the internet every day. We use it to talk to friends, watch videos, and find information. The internet helps us connect with others. For example, we can send messages or make video calls. This is easy and fast.

Many people like to shop online. They can buy clothes, food, and books from home. Online shopping is popular because it saves time. People can find good prices and special deals.

Students also use the internet for learning. They can find information for school projects. There are many educational websites. Students can watch videos to learn new things.

However, we must be careful. There are some dangers on the internet. Not all information is good or true. It is important to check sources and not share personal information with strangers.

In summary, the internet is a big part of our lives. It helps us connect, shop, and learn. We should use it wisely.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do people use the internet for?
questions.explanation: People use the internet to talk to friends as mentioned in the passage.

questions.text: The internet is not useful for shopping.
questions.explanation: The passage states that many people shop online, making the statement false.

questions.text: Students can find information for school projects online.
questions.explanation: The passage says students use the internet for learning and finding information for projects.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What should we do when using the internet?
questions.explanation: The passage advises checking sources to stay safe on the internet.

questions.text: What can students do to learn new things?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions students can watch videos to learn new things.

vocabulary.meaning: to join or link with others

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

Read the passage carefully. Look for keywords in the questions. Check the passage to find the answers directly. Remember, some questions ask for true or false information, so pay attention to what the text says.
